content.body: Is there some environment (Node.js vm module?) where you can have a module importing a module from a different realm?
I'm reading InitializeEnvironment(), and it seems to me that if module A does `import * as ns from "B"`, B's namespace object is created in A's Realm
content.formatted_body: I'm reading InitializeEnvironment(), and it seems to me that if module A does import * as ns from "B", B's namespace object is created in A's Realm

content.body: Not sure if it's observable, as they don't have a prototype
https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/25443db104d39a3dcd410fa882884580734cfaa4/lib/internal/vm/module.js#L354-L356 Node.js `vm` right now does not allow importing a module from a different realm
content.formatted_body: https://github.com/nodejs/node/blob/25443db104d39a3dcd410fa882884580734cfaa4/lib/internal/vm/module.js#L354-L356 Node.js vm right now does not allow importing a module from a different realm
